# Source-to-Rule Matrix

<!-- Generated by tools/generate_rule_matrix.py. Do not edit manually. -->

The rule registry is canonical. Source support is bounded by each audited card's recorded access scope.

| Rule | Status | Layer | Imperative | Supporting sources | Counterconditions |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `MGR-001` | `official_outlet_guidance` | `managerial_framing` | Center the frame on a consequential choice owned by a named actor. | `hbr-contributor-guidelines` (tier_a_official_guidance_or_editorial) | Exploratory framing may leave the actor provisional but must mark it unknown. |
| `MGR-002` | `original_framework_rule` | `managerial_framing` | Explain the mechanism and structural trade-off before recommending action. |  | A descriptive study may identify a phenomenon before a causal mechanism is established. |
| `EVD-001` | `official_outlet_guidance` | `evidence` | Support importance claims with traceable evidence or label them as unverified. | `hbr-contributor-guidelines` (tier_a_official_guidance_or_editorial) | Purely synthetic illustrations may state modeled stakes without claiming empirical prevalence. |
| `SCH-001` | `core_triangulated` | `scholarly_positioning` | Describe scholarly contribution in relation to prior literature and state its claimed originality and utility without treating the claim as proof. | `corley-gioia-2011` (tier_b_peer_reviewed_foundational); `locke-golden-biddle-1997` (tier_b_peer_reviewed_foundational) | A context can contribute when it changes a mechanism boundary construct or inference and that change is demonstrated. |
| `SCH-002` | `provisional_single_source` | `scholarly_positioning` | Consider challenging a literature assumption as one route to formulating a research question, and do not promise influence. | `alvesson-sandberg-2011` (tier_b_peer_reviewed_foundational) | A documented omission can matter when its consequence and mechanism are made explicit. |
| `SCH-003` | `original_framework_rule` | `prose` | Organize introductions flexibly from territory and tension to question, contribution, and bounded relevance. |  | Section order should adapt to genre audience research design and the evidence supplied. |
| `IMP-001` | `original_framework_rule` | `managerial_framing` | Express each managerial implication through actor, decision, condition, mechanism, consequence, and implementation requirement. |  | Exploratory outputs may identify a pathway as missing instead of supplying an implication. |
| `OR-001` | `original_framework_rule` | `managerial_framing` | Link analytical or computational performance to a decision deadline and operational pathway before claiming value. |  | A paper may claim computational improvement alone when it does not claim managerial impact. |
| `IS-001` | `original_framework_rule` | `fidelity` | Distinguish latent types, observable signals, system actions, private objectives, and welfare outcomes. |  | A type can be treated as observed only when the research specification supplies a valid observation process. |
| `PRAC-001` | `exemplar_observation` | `evidence` | Treat practitioner exemplars as bounded execution observations, never as universal scientific authority. | `thomke-manzi-2014` (tier_c_exemplar_execution); `rawson-duncan-jones-2013` (tier_c_exemplar_execution); `drucker-2002` (tier_c_exemplar_execution) | A separately verified empirical source may support a broader claim on its own terms. |

## Coverage

- `hbr-contributor-guidelines`: `MGR-001`, `EVD-001`
- `thomke-manzi-2014`: `PRAC-001`
- `rawson-duncan-jones-2013`: `PRAC-001`
- `drucker-2002`: `PRAC-001`
- `davis-1971`: no admitted rule; retained for bounded synthesis
- `whetten-1989`: no admitted rule; retained for bounded synthesis
- `sutton-staw-1995`: no admitted rule; retained for bounded synthesis
- `locke-golden-biddle-1997`: `SCH-001`
- `alvesson-sandberg-2011`: `SCH-002`
- `corley-gioia-2011`: `SCH-001`
- `gregor-hevner-2013`: no admitted rule; retained for bounded synthesis
- `rai-type-iii-errors`: no admitted rule; retained for bounded synthesis
- `straub-ang-rigor-relevance`: no admitted rule; retained for bounded synthesis
- `tang-2016`: no admitted rule; retained for bounded synthesis

- `swaminathan-2023`: no admitted rule; retained for bounded synthesis
